recommendations: add a minimal smoke-test suite for the MCP plugin (server startup
each endpoint callable
import-mode failures surfaced clearly)
wire MCP tests into GitHub Actions with a small OS/Python matrix
fix dependency detection in your analysis pipeline (it missed existing pyproject.toml and requirements.txt)
pin and validate MCP runtime deps (fastmcp/pydantic) with upper/lower bounds and a lock/constraints file
add structured error mapping in adapter.py so phonopy exceptions become stable MCP error codes/messages
introduce pydantic request/response schemas per endpoint (especially file I/O endpoints) to enforce input validation
add timeout and resource guards for heavy workflows (mesh/DOS/QHA) to prevent long-running MCP calls
implement endpoint-level logging/trace IDs and optional debug mode for reproducible support
add golden integration tests using small fixtures from test/ and example/ (e.g.
NaCl/Si) to verify numerical output shapes and key invariants
improve README_MCP.md with concrete per-endpoint examples and expected artifacts
add version compatibility checks at MCP startup (phonopy
numpy
spglib) with actionable warnings
split very large adapter surface into domain modules (api/interface/io) to reduce maintenance risk
add mypy/ruff/pre-commit config to mcp_output for static quality gates
define semantic versioning and changelog for the MCP plugin independently from upstream phonopy
add security hardening for file-path inputs (normalize paths
deny traversal
optional sandbox root)
expose a lightweight health endpoint reporting plugin version/import status/dependency availability

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal status 'success' and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Robust source acquisition fallback (zip import) mitigated git clone SSL timeout and enabled analysis of ~315 files.
Import-mode adapter strategy matched repository characteristics (high import feasibility 0.9, low intrusiveness risk).
Generated MCP scaffold and service startup/health checks passed (stdio transport healthy).
failure_reasons: Primary repository clone failed due to network/SSL timeout to GitHub.
Static dependency/entry-point detection produced false negatives (reported missing pyproject/requirements despite presence).
Quality metrics are partially non-observable (0 files processed, 0 LOC, empty code review, no performance telemetry), reducing confidence.
Original project test execution did not pass/run meaningfully (passed=false with empty details and 0s timing), so integration correctness is not fully validated.

node_performance.download_time: Degraded by clone failure, but recovered via zip fallback; resilience good, network robustness needs improvement.
node_performance.analysis_time: Substantial repository scan completed (large tree, medium complexity) but metadata extraction quality inconsistent.
node_performance.generation_time: Efficient MCP scaffold generation (7 files) with practical endpoint surface inferred from core APIs.
node_performance.test_time: Plugin smoke validation passed, but original project and endpoint-level behavioral tests are insufficient for confidence.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0 MB likely placeholder); cannot assess accurately.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown/insufficient telemetry (reported 0% likely placeholder); profiling instrumentation needed.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Moderate and likely efficient for scaffold output, but generated size metrics are missing/zeroed.
